name: Stephanie Schriock, EMILY's List
subject: They ran. And they won.
body:
We elect pro-choice Democratic women who will fight for women and families. | [ ](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6ccb/24c69fd9/16e654db8/189ee932/1727536473/VEsH/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/donate-to-emily?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=1+-+%0D%0A++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++&utm_campaign=WQEFEK20N_digital_email_20171107_ElectionNightWins_inact&source=WQEFEK20N_digital_email_20171107_ElectionNightWins_inact")
---

Peter,

Results are still coming in, but we Democrats have a LOT to celebrate tonight.

So far Democrats have:
* Secured governorships in Virginia and New Jersey, including New Jersey Lieutenant Governor-elect Sheila Oliver

* Elected **at least** 12 pro-choice Democratic women to the Virginia House of Delegates, including
* Hala Ayala and Elizabeth Guzman, the FIRST Latinas elected to the House of Delegates
* Danica Roem, the FIRST openly transgender person elected to the Virginia General Assembly
* Kathy Tran, the FIRST Vietnamese American elected to the House of Delegates

* Elected a historic number of women of color to the Boston City Council, including our endorsed candidates Lydia Edwards and Kim Janey

This is what happens when Democrats unite against hate. When we all put everything we have on the field, WE WIN.

This is just the beginning. I am so proud of our candidates who had the courage to run. I am so grateful to our partners who rallied to help these women win.

And I am so grateful to you, Peter. These wins belong to the EMILY's List community, too.

**If you want to keep winning in the months to come, donate $3 right now to help build MORE strong campaigns for pro-choice Democratic women:**

**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/How-We-Win](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6ccb/24c69fd9/16e654db8/189ee933/1727536473/VEsE/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20171107_email_ElectionNightWins?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgHowWeWin&utm_campaign=WQEFEK20N_digital_email_20171107_ElectionNightWins_inact&source=WQEFEK20N_digital_email_20171107_ElectionNightWins_inact")**

Thanks,

Stephanie Schriock
President, EMILY's List

name: Democrats.org
subject: Fwd: This is just the beginning
body:
Democrats won BIG last night!
---
Last night was a historic win for us, Derek. We walked away with victories in Virginia, New Jersey, and beyond.

Those wins weren't a fluke or just a reaction to Donald Trump. They're what happens when we lead with a strong ground game, harness the latest tools and technology, and invest in Democrats at ALL levels -- from the school board to the Oval Office.

So if you think last night's wins were something, take a minute and think of what we can accomplish in Alabama's Senate special election and in 2018.

**Then chip in $10 or more to keep winning and elect more Democrats:

** **[DONATE](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/405239cd/4067b0a/40e520872/7e5958e2/134389009/VEsH/ "https://my.democrats.org/Elect-Democrats-2017-AB?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+DONATE&utm_campaign=em_20171108_joc_nd&source=em_20171108_joc_nd&refcode=em_20171108_joc_nd")**

Thanks,

Jess

Jess O'Connell
Chief Executive Officer
Democratic National Committee

\---------- Forwarded message ----------
Sender: Tom Perez
Date: Tue, Nov 7, 2017
Subject: This is just the beginning

Because of you, Democrats just won big tonight in Virginia and New Jersey! What we're doing is working.

This election cycle, the Democratic National Committee invested more in boots on the ground and grassroots and digital organizing than in any "off-year" before. Good old-fashioned organizing paired with the latest technology and tools helped put our candidates over the top.

I am so proud of the campaigns run by Virginia's Ralph Northam, Justin Fairfax, and Mark Herring, New Jersey's Phil Murphy and Sheila Oliver, and Democrats all over the country, up and down the ballot. These candidates worked hard day in and day out fighting to represent their states, and I know that they will take that same spirit and fight into their terms.

But here's the thing: All of this is just the beginning.

We are going to keep investing in state parties and supporting Democrats from the school board to the Oval Office. And if we continue to channel our energy into powering this movement, there's no doubt in my mind that we will see wins like this in 2018, 2020, and beyond.

**If you're even half as fired up as I am, chip in $10 or more right now to keep electing Democrats.**

I am so proud of the campaigns run by Virginia's Ralph Northam, Justin Fairfax, and Mark Herring, New Jersey's Phil Murphy and Sheila Oliver, and Democrats all over the country, up and down the ballot. These candidates worked hard day in and day out fighting to represent their states, and I know that they will take that same spirit and fight into their terms. But here's the thing: All of this is just the beginning. We are going to keep investing in state parties and supporting Democrats from the school board to the Oval Office. And if we continue to channel our energy into powering this movement, there's no doubt in my mind that we will see wins like this in 2018, 2020, and beyond. If you're even half as fired up as I am, chip in $10 or more right now to keep electing Democrats. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately. "" "" "" "" "" "" Thanks. Keep up the hard work and let's keep winning. Tom Tom Perez Chair Democratic National Committee Paid for by the Democratic National Committee, 430 South Capitol Street SE, Washington DC 20003 and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. subject: Stop Jeff Sessions’ Drug War
body:

=================================
Escalating the failed war on drugs?

Sign the Petition.
=================================
ADD YOUR NAME: https://engage.drugpolicy.org/secure/dems-sessions-rescind-your-draconian-memo?ms=2A1_1709DemsAcq2&utm_campaign=fy18democrats&utm_source=1709demsonacq2&utm_medium=email&cid=70141000000f1zZAAQ
=================================
Dear Activist,

By using Regan-era rhetoric and implementing draconian drug war policies, the current administration is trying to take us back in time.

In a disheartening reversal of Obama-era progress, Attorney General Jeff Sessions issued a memo calling on prosecutors to pursue the "most serious" sentences for drug offenses, even nonviolent ones.

Tell the Department of Justice that it's time to end the war on drugs, not escalate it.
=================================
ADD YOUR NAME: https://engage.drugpolicy.org/secure/dems-sessions-rescind-your-draconian-memo?ms=2A1_1709DemsAcq2&utm_campaign=fy18democrats&utm_source=1709demsonacq2&utm_medium=email&cid=70141000000f1zZAAQ
=================================
We have fought too hard to allow Sessions to unravel our progress. Together we worked hard to legalize recreational and medical marijuana, reform the criminal justice system, and enact harm reduction and overdose prevention policies. We still have a long way to go, and the growing national opioid crisis is just another extreme example of how prohibition does nothing to reduce drug use or increase public safety. Instead, it increases the prison population and exacerbates racial disparities in the criminal justice system.
=================================

name: The White House
subject: Advancing American and Global Prosperity
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=09c2a2c1299bc60220cd4037a80e6e8867107979a367dc6e4def14c070b3a30e0f0d18846465edf42c45456c4a2792ed45af819f47c86bd7

America has seen a return to 3 percent GDP growth in the last two quarters, but the current tax code hinders American job-creators and makes U.S. businesses uncompetitive world-wide. Our country is poised for historic economic growth by reducing the complexity of our current tax code and promoting an economic environment that motivates American companies to reinvest their overseas wealth back home. We can lay a prosperous foundation for years to come by insisting on a tax system that ensures Americans keep more of their hard-earned money.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=09c2a2c1299bc602fdbe133c961965539071ab336935a713446be814b6f45f0216dc88e7c0a1f6b4b34fb860b23d430e25782e13ab609394
Learn more about pro-job, pro-family, and pro-America tax reform.

---------------------

United States and Republic of Korea Stand Unified in Denuclearizing North Korea

Last night in Seoul, President Trump delivered remarks to the National Assembly of the Republic of Korea to address the security challenges presented by North Korea.

“The time for excuses is over. Now is the time for strength. If you want peace, you must stand strong at all times. The world cannot tolerate the menace of a rogue regime that threatens with nuclear devastation.

All responsible nations must join forces to isolate the brutal regime of North Korea -- to deny it and any form -- any form of it. You cannot support, you cannot supply, you cannot accept. We call on every nation, including China and Russia, to fully implement U.N. Security Council resolutions, downgrade diplomatic relations with the regime, and sever all ties of trade and technology.

It is our responsibility and our duty to confront this danger together -- because the longer we wait, the greater the danger grows, and the fewer the options become. And to those nations that choose to ignore this threat, or, worse still, to enable it, the weight of this crisis is on your conscience,” said the President.

See more from President Trump’s speech
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=09c2a2c1299bc602d897f4ba5ef88b130ea7fcf7778b4652c7dcf4829067a659ee4dcac12dc3a2b9a46cecdec1bdb9aacf6ed5646b488230
here.

First Lady Melania Trump Kicks Off Campaign for Girls in Republic of Korea

This week, in Seoul, Republic of Korea, First Lady Melania Trump helped kick off the United States Embassy’s “Girls Play 2!” campaign, which is a program that garners support for the 2018 Winter Olympics and encourages girls’ increased participation in sports. President Moon and Mrs. Kim warmly welcomed President Trump and Mrs. Trump to the Blue House, where the First Ladies participated in tea and a friendship walk before their husbands’ joint press conference. The evening ended with a state banquet at the Blue House.

On her second day in Seoul, Mrs. Trump greeted employees at the U.S. Embassy, then accompanied her husband to his speech at the National Assembly and a wreath laying ceremony at the National Cemetery.
“I was honored by the warm welcome we received in the Republic of Korea,” First Lady Melania Trump said. “I very much enjoyed the conversations I had with Mrs. Kim and was impressed by the children I had the opportunity to meet. I look forward to continuing this friendship and extend my continued support to the people of the Republic of Korea.”

Read more about the First Lady’s visit
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=09c2a2c1299bc60238b6f8fa12e9370a8bcd51f6cdc4aa408b7b56d0e4a7225e0bb930f8a28886b74a79ea3f702a435fe1b0c7f70b4c1d22
here.

Vice President Pence Reflects on Historic First Year in Office

Vice President Mike Pence penned an op-ed in USA Today celebrating one year since President Donald Trump’s historic election on November 8, 2017.
“It’s been a year of action. It’s been a year of results. And it’s been a year that will be remembered as the time we began to Make America Great Again,” the Vice President wrote, surveying the numerous accomplishments of the Trump Administration so far.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=09c2a2c1299bc602d15aa6716ff9b5dae9a1150838e8bd23f9c74f67efd1eca3d7a48d167fe2dedcbb17bd5487bebd00424d1fca146ba8a6
Read the Vice President’s full op-ed.

Photo of the Day

President Donald J. Trump and First Lady Melania Trump participate in a welcoming ceremony in South Korea | November 7, 2017 (Official White House Photo by Andrea Hanks)

POTUS TODAY

Today, President Trump and First Lady Melania Trump will participate in a welcome ceremony with President Xi Jinping of the People’s Republic of China and Madame Peng Liyuan. Then, President Trump will participate in bilateral meetings with President Xi, followed by a business event, and a joint press statement. President Trump will then meet with Premier Li Keqiang of the People’s Republic of China. Later, President Trump and the First Lady will participate in an embassy meet and greet, attend a state dinner, and view a cultural performance.

Vice President Mike Pence and Second Lady Karen Pence are traveling to San Antonio, Texas. There, the Vice President and Second Lady, joined by Governor Greg Abbott (R-TX), will visit victims of the First Baptist Church shooting at a local hospital. The Vice President will then participate in a briefing with local law enforcement officials in Sutherland Springs, Texas. In the evening, the Vice President and Second Lady will meet with the families of the victims and participate in a prayer vigil honoring those who lost their lives in the First Baptist Church shooting.

COMING UP

President Trump continues traveling in Asia this week. After concluding his trip to China, the President will visit Vietnam and the Philippines. Vice President Mike Pence will visit Plainfield, Indiana, to speak on tax reform on Thursday.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=09c2a2c1299bc602fda8520e065f4a971846417f27be68c9b22b8c1866b4cc60bfcb1a3232fab11fa949779df307af93f230f1a3f9612b30
Privacy Policy |

name: John Kasich
subject: Two paths
body:
While campaigning for President in 2016, I made a speech titled,
"Two Paths," where I stated my belief that Americans ultimately
needed to come together to solve our country's problems.

Peter, while campaigning for
President in 2016, I made a speech titled, "Two Paths," where I
stated my belief that Americans ultimately needed to come
together to solve our country's problems.

In light of Tuesday's election
results, I wanted to share a short passage from that speech with
you as I believe it is more important than ever today:

"Two paths.

One choice - the path that
exploits anger, encourages resentment, turns fear into hatred and
divides people.

This path solves nothing, demeans
our history, weakens our country and cheapens each of us.

It has but one beneficiary and
that is to the politician who speaks of it.

The other path is the one America
has been down before.

It is well trod, it is at times
steep, but it is solid.

It is the same path our forebears
took together.

It is from this higher path that
we are offered the greater view.

And, imagine for a moment with me
that view.

Fear turns to hope because we
remember to take strength from each other.

Uncertainty turns to peace
because we reclaim our faith in the American ideals that have
carried us upward before.

And America's supposed decline
becomes its finest hour, because we came together to say "no" to
those who would prey on our human weakness and instead chose
leadership that serves, helping us look up, not down.

This is the path I believe in.

This is the America I believe in.

And, this is the America I know
all Americans want us to be."

--

Thank you for all that you're
doing to help spread this important message!

-John

Derek --

Congratulations to Ralph Northam, Justin Fairfax, and Mark Herring! Our Commonwealth has spoken loud and clear: Virginia is for lovers.

Democrats are fired up -- and now is the time to keep moving ahead. Let's make sure Ralph has a progressive partner in Washington, D.C. next year. Become a founding donor of Tim Kaine's re-election campaign.

https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tk-founding-donor?refcode=20171108&amount=25

DONATE

https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tk-founding-donor?refcode=20171108&amount=25

Last night represented a major win for Virginia, and not just because we will continue to have strong Democratic leadership in Richmond. It's also a victory because of just how much folks all across the Commonwealth stepped up for our shared values by knocking on doors, making phone calls, and pitching in where they could.

We're ready to keep fighting for progress by fighting to keep Tim Kaine in the Senate. If you are too, you can own a piece of this effort by chipping in and becoming a Team Kaine founding donor.

https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tk-founding-donor?refcode=20171108&amount=25

Thank you -- onward!

-- Team Kaine

name: Jay Inslee
subject: One year in -- still fighting
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jay Inslee for Washington
body:
We need to stay strong against Trump's climate attacks.

| [](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=5&ms=MTIzMTAyMzQS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTI4MDY5MTYyNAS2&mt=1&rt=0) Friend, Do you remember where you were when the 2016 election was called? I know I do. One year ago today, Donald Trump was proclaimed the next president. It's been a long, tough year. One of America's top climate deniers has lived up to his terrible campaign rhetoric, and we've endured one dystopian headline after the next:
* Trump selected Scott Pruitt to lead the EPA, and Pruitt spent the next year meeting almost **exclusively with dirty coal and big oil lobbyists**.
* America withdrew from the historic and critical **Paris climate agreement**.
* The **Clean Power Plan has been repealed** , the cornerstone of Obama's effort to fight climate change by curbing greenhouse gas emissions.

Inch by inch, Trump is unwinding the progress made under the Obama administration towards a more sustainable future. Now more than ever, we must stand up for climate action. [**A year later, sign on and stand with me as we take on Trump 's climate attacks. Let the world know you're on our team -- committed to a clean energy agenda.**](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=4&ms=MTIzMTAyMzQS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTI4MDY5MTYyNAS2&mt=1&rt=0) For climate leaders like us, this year has been incredibly painful to watch. But I'm not going to take this sitting down. I won't give up and I know you won't either. We must defend our natural resources and slow the threat of climate change before it's too late. In Washington, we're taking climate action by making this state a global leader in clean energy. In the wake of President Trump's announcement that the United States is withdrawing from the Paris agreement, it's on state governments to pick up the mantle of climate leadership and demonstrate that the country will continue to do its part. So here's my request of you -- I want your support. If the world sees us leading from the state level, we'll have even more power to make our environmental dreams a reality. | [SIGN ON NOW »](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=6&ms=MTIzMTAyMzQS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTI4MDY5MTYyNAS2&mt=1&rt=0)
---
We will not allow our country to be sold by this president and his billionaire cabinet to corporations intent on destroying our natural legacy. Trump may be doing all he can to roll back progress, but together, we can stop him.
Very truly yours,
Jay Inslee

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. [Click here to unsubscribe](http://www.jayinslee.com/unsubscribe?m=12310234&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&j=MTI4MDY5MTYyNAS2&l=7535981&e=dpwillis67@gmail.com). name: Tim Kaine
subject: Recounts
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
Derek --

I'm so proud of Virginia. When I first ran for statewide office in 2001, all statewide officeholders were Republican, and we were deep red in presidential politics. Today, Democrats have won 10 statewide races in a row. All of our statewide elected officials are Democrats. We've gone blue for three presidential races in a row. Four of the last five Governors have been Democrats. And we've had huge wins in the House of Delegates.

**But this is really important: We are so close to flipping the House of Delegates into Democratic control -- and six races from last night's election are likely to go into recounts.**

That means if just three of those six are officially called for Democrats, we will gain a majority and be able to take decisive action on issues like Medicaid expansion and redistricting reform. **[But Democrats need your help to fund these recounts. Will you chip in right now to make sure we have the resources we need?](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=yfyX6jYZ%2BqZDf5Hw8wvjLAXEjYuIcFCf)**

This is just incredible. Virginia is the biggest turnaround in recent American politics, and a Democratic majority in the House of Delegates would push us into solidly blue territory.

The secret to our success? Good jobs, education, health care for all. We fight for everyday people instead of fighting each other, and we wholeheartedly reject divisive Trump-style politics.

**[Please pitch in right now to help fund these critical recounts.](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=GEwbj5jUJldRoZHI9bDikAXEjYuIcFCf)**

Thank you so much.

Tim Kaine

name: Team Gillum
subject: No regrets
body:
|
---
| | Hello --

Democrats have a lot to be optimistic about heading into 2018 -- and it showed in elections across the country! Last night, we were dreaming big -- because that's what you do when you stand up and fight for who and what you believe in.

With progressive wins in Virginia, New Jersey, and the re-election of Mayor Rick Kriseman in St. Petersburg, it's clear that voters are hungry for progress and a brighter, more inclusive future. A future that reminds us that we're all in this together.

A year ago we were waking up to the reality that Donald Trump was elected as our 45th President, and we've seen the disastrous repercussions from that election almost every day since.

And in Florida, we've had 20 years of moving in the wrong direction -- and last night's elections tell us that we're ready to take our state back. We've had enough of the non-stop attacks on access to affordable health care, and the tax breaks to the top 1% at the expense of working families. We've had enough of the inaction on climate change, and enough of the attacks on public schools and teachers.

President Trump and his allies have done nothing but try and roll back the progress we've made as a country in the eight years prior. And sadly, we don't have a leader in the Governor's Mansion willing to stand up to him and put our families ahead of partisan politics.

We have the opportunity to turn things around here in Florida -- it starts with people just like you, and our campaign is ready to fight with you to move Florida forward. In 2018, we want to replicate the big dreams and happiness we felt last night. In 2018, we want to wake up with hope for a better future.

name: =?utf-8?Q?Eric=20Holcomb=20for=20Indiana?=
subject: Gov. Holcomb's 2018 Legislative Agenda
body:
| | | |
---
| | |
# 2018 Next Level Agenda
Today Governor Eric Holcomb unveiled his 2018 Next Level Legislative Agenda. Read more about his priorities for next year's legislative session, with a focus on preparing Indiana's workforce to fill high-demand, high-wage jobs now & in the future.
---
|
---
|
---
|
---
| "With nearly 29,000 new jobs announced this year and one million expected over the next decade, we must act now to ensure Hoosiers have the skills they need to secure good jobs and succeed in our growing economy." -Governor Eric Holcomb
---
|
---
| | **Legislative Agenda Pillars:

_Economic Growth_** Cultivate a strong and diverse economy

**_Infrastructure_**
Maintain & build Indiana's infrastructure

**_Workforce_**
Develop a 21st century skilled & ready workforce

**_Drug Epidemic_**
Attack the drug epidemic
**_Great Government_**
Deliver great government service
---
|
---
| [Read More](https://facebook.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80a71c2714359455775d068a2&id=b29124a804&e=716de7d363 "Read More")
---
| [Watch the Press Conference](https://facebook.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80a71c2714359455775d068a2&id=0c098b420b&e=716de7d363 "Watch the Press Conference")
---
|
---
| |
---
### Cultivate a Strong & Diverse Economy
* Prepare for the next generation of transportation by authorizing testing and operation of autonomous vehicles
* Support Indiana's tech sector by clarifying the sales tax treatment of Software as a Service
* Market Indiana's story to expand economic opportunity & attract new talent
* Encourage communities to plan and collaborate through the best elements of the Indiana Regional Cities Initiative and the Stellar Communities program
* Expand recruitment efforts to attract and relocate more veterans to Indiana when they transition out of service

---
|
---
| |
---
### Maintain & Build Indiana's Infrastructure
* Expand Indiana's global presence by building a fourth port
* Develop strategies for management of the state's water resources
* Increase the availability of and access to affordable broadband services
* Attract more domestic and international flights
* Move forward with priority infrastructure projects

---
|
---
| |
---
### Develop a 21st Century Skilled & Ready Workforce
* Create the Governor's Education to Career Pathway Cabinet, helping local leaders build and align training to local needs
* Require every Indiana school to offer computer science courses by 2021
* Create stronger career pathways for high school juniors and seniors
* Strengthen STEM curriculum in Indiana schools
* Increase teacher license flexibility in tech, STEM and career curricula
* Align Workforce Investment Boards directly to job placement and wage outcomes
* Incentivize and measure career planning and placement at higher education institutions
* Connect working-age adults with training that leads directly to employment
* Offer Department of Corrections offenders training that prepares them for high-quality job opportunities and reduces recidivism

---
| |
---
### Attack the Drug Epidemic
* Strengthen enforcement actions with new charges for drug dealing & illicit drug manufacturing that result in drug-induced death
* Improve health outcomes by requiring physicians to check Indiana's prescription drug monitoring program before issuing prescriptions for opioids
* Standardize the reporting of drug overdose deaths to increase consistency and understanding of the crisis's scope
* Increase the number of FSSA-approved opioid treatment locations
* Increase drug treatment options by initiating a state referral process, linking patients to addiction treatment options
* Lower the infant mortality rate by implementing Levels of Care certification at hospitals and birthing centers

---
| |
---
### Deliver Great Government Service
* Streamline & simplify how citizens conduct their business with the state by creating a single online sign-on
* Improve management and delivery of large-scale information technology projects with more rigorous project reviews
* Create a new parent leave policy for state employees that promotes talent retention, talent recruitment, family formation and involved parents
* Continue strong fiscal management & maintain our reserves at a prudent level
* Work with elementary students to adpot the Say's Firefly as the state insect

---
|
---
| [Learn More](https://facebook.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80a71c2714359455775d068a2&id=9b5f425ca1&e=716de7d363 "Learn More")

name: Stephanie Schriock, EMILY's List
subject: [Update] A really, REALLY good night for Democrats
body:
We elect pro-choice Democratic women who will fight for women and families. | [ ](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6cc6/24c69fd9/16e9c1639/189ee6d8/1770055776/VEsH/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/donate-to-emily?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=1+-+%0D%0A++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++&utm_campaign=WQEFEK22N_digital_email_20171108_ElectionNightWinsKick_inact&source=WQEFEK22N_digital_email_20171108_ElectionNightWinsKick_inact")
---

Peter,

You may have been asleep when I sent my last email, but here's what you need to know:

It was a really, _really_ good night for Democrats. **And it was a GREAT night for pro-choice Democratic women.**

In Democrats' sweeping victory in Virginia, we flipped **11** House of Delegates seats with EMILY's List candidates. And we could still add more because Shelly Simonds' race is too close to call.

Manka Dhingra, an immigrant and mental health advocate, won her special election in Washington State, successfully turning the state Senate there blue. And Deborah Gonzalez turned Georgia's state House District 117 blue, too!

Across the country at the city, county, and state level, Democrats are still counting up our wins, many of them featuring history-making women, immigrants, people of color, and the LGBTQ community.

**We won these races because we put the resources behind our candidates to win. Donate $3 to help our women who are running in 2018 build strong campaigns that can win, too:**

**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/How-We-Win](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6cc6/24c69fd9/16e9c1639/189ee6d9/1770055776/VEsE/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20171107_email_ElectionNightWins?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgHowWeWin&utm_campaign=WQEFEK22N_digital_email_20171108_ElectionNightWinsKick_inact&source=WQEFEK22N_digital_email_20171108_ElectionNightWinsKick_inact")**

Thanks,

Stephanie Schriock
President, EMILY's List

_\-----------Original Message-----------
From: Stephanie Schriock, EMILY's List
Sent: Wed, Nov 8, 2017 at 12:32 a.m._

Peter,

Results are still coming in, but we Democrats have a LOT to celebrate tonight.

So far Democrats have:
* Secured governorships in Virginia and New Jersey, including New Jersey Lieutenant Governor-elect Sheila Oliver

* Elected **at least** 12 pro-choice Democratic women to the Virginia House of Delegates, including
* Hala Ayala and Elizabeth Guzman, the FIRST Latinas elected to the House of Delegates
* Danica Roem, the FIRST openly transgender person elected to the Virginia General Assembly
* Kathy Tran, the FIRST Vietnamese American elected to the House of Delegates

* Elected a historic number of women of color to the Boston City Council, including our endorsed candidates Lydia Edwards and Kim Janey

This is what happens when Democrats unite against hate. When we all put everything we have on the field, WE WIN.

This is just the beginning. I am so proud of our candidates who had the courage to run. I am so grateful to our partners who rallied to help these women win.

And I am so grateful to you, Peter. These wins belong to the EMILY's List community, too.

**If you want to keep winning in the months to come, donate $3 right now to help build MORE strong campaigns for pro-choice Democratic women:**

**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/How-We-Win](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6cc6/24c69fd9/16e9c1639/189ee6da/1770055776/VEsF/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20171107_email_ElectionNightWins?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=3+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgHowWeWin&utm_campaign=WQEFEK22N_digital_email_20171108_ElectionNightWinsKick_inact&source=WQEFEK22N_digital_email_20171108_ElectionNightWinsKick_inact")**

Thanks,

Stephanie Schriock
President, EMILY's List

clean_body: We elect pro-choice Democratic women who will fight for women and families. | "" Peter, You may have been asleep when I sent my last email, but here's what you need to know: It was a really, really good night for Democrats. And we could still add more because Shelly Simonds' race is too close to call. Manka Dhingra, an immigrant and mental health advocate, won her special election in Washington State, successfully turning the state Senate there blue. And Deborah Gonzalez turned Georgia's state House District 117 blue, too! Across the country at the city, county, and state level, Democrats are still counting up our wins, many of them featuring history-making women, immigrants, people of color, and the LGBTQ community. We won these races because we put the resources behind our candidates to win. name: Chris Van Hollen
subject: keep the momentum going
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Van Hollen for US Senate
body:
| __
Yesterday was like the Return of the Jedi! We are starting to take our country back -- one city, one state at a time. Effective, progressive Democratic candidates defeated Republicans in mayoral races in Annapolis and Frederick. And around the country -- in Virgina, New Jersey, Washington State, Georgia New Hampshire, and many other places -- Democrats scored major victories.

But our fight isn't over - it's essential that we keep this momentum going. In less than one month Democratic nominee Doug Jones will be on the ballot against alt-right icon Roy Moore in the Alabama special election for U.S. Senate. And that's only the beginning: the critical 2018 midterm elections are now officially just ONE YEAR AWAY.

[**We need as many supporters as possible to commit to being part of the push for victory in 2018. Will you help us build our resources for this fight by giving a donation today to become a Senate Sustainer? Our goal is to sign up 250 new Senate Sustainers by the end of this week >>**](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91bbe/70cdc2ba/46b8c0f0/397d1d9/1697114041/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/senatesustainer?refcode=em110817&amount=18.00&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=1+-+We+need+as+many+supporters+as+possible+t&utm_campaign=em110817&source=em110817")
**[Become a monthly Senate Sustainer!](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91bbe/70cdc2ba/46b8c0f0/397d1d6/1697114041/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/senatesustainer_monthly_?refcode=em110817&amount=18.00&recurring=auto&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=2+-+Become+a+monthly+Senate+Sustainer&utm_campaign=em110817&source=em110817")**
To understand the stakes of next year's Congressional elections you need to look no further than the GOP's current "tax reform" bill. This legislation, like the GOP health bill before it, is a massive giveaway to the wealthy and powerful, at the expense of everyone else.

Just like Trumpcare, the Republican tax plan demonstrates a total lack of concern for the impact on millions of hardworking Americans who will lose critical deductions and be left with a massive $1.5 trillion national debt -- which sooner, rather than later, will lead to drastic cuts to critical programs like Medicaid and Social Security, that they rely on.

As Chair of the Democratic Senatorial Campaign Committee, I am working to make sure Senate Democrats have the strongest campaigns they possibly can to push back against the attack ads of shady GOP Super PACs and compete to win big next November. But I can't do this alone.

[**Please click here and become part of this important one year campaign to victory in the 2018 Elections. You can help us reach our goal by signing up now and your help will ensure we are prepared for the tough road ahead.**](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91bbe/70cdc2ba/46b8c0f0/397d1d7/1697114041/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/senatesustainer?refcode=em110817&amount=18.00&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=3+-+Please+click+here+and+become+part+of+thi&utm_campaign=em110817&source=em110817")

As always, thank you for your continued friendship and support on this journey,

Chris Van Hollen U.S. Senator

name: Peter
subject: So, about last night 🔥
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Donnelly for Indiana
body:
I'm going to be honest: it feels good to have momentum back on our side. |
---

| | Peter, I'm going to be honest: **it feels good to have momentum back on our side**. It's exciting to see decisive victories across America like we did last night. But more importantly, it shows the strength of our country, and it reminds us that being right on the issues is more important to voters of both parties than whose rhetoric can be more divisive.

**But yesterday was just the beginning.** I'm an organizer at heart, and I see this as an opportunity to build on this moment and bring the fight right here to Indiana. In order to win, we have to compete in every county, fill the ballot to contest every race, recruit first-time candidates, and of course, make sure Joe stays in the Senate.

But to do that, we have to start organizing now -- and that means opening field offices and hiring a grassroots staff to take today's momentum forward. **Can you chip in $10 or $25 today to keep help get our field program off the ground so we can keep moving forward to 2018?** | _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_

****[Or, donate a different amount>>](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c2560/7377f838/3520c5af/51fbab3a/248133024/VEsD/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-donate?refcode=20171108nd")****
Peter, the victories in New Jersey, Virginia and across the country are going to be spun a lot of different ways, but there's one thing you should take away -- the community organizing that started with friends and neighbors knocking on each others' doors a year before election day had a huge impact. **Believe it or not, there 's not a lot of time between yesterday's election and November 2018, and there's no shortage of work to do!**

Let's keep moving forward. **[Join our grassroots action team by donating $10 or $25 to our campaign, and let's get to work sharing Joe's message of hard work, common sense and Hoosier values.](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c2560/7377f838/3520c5af/51fbab35/248133024/VEsA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-donate?refcode=20171108nd&amount=10")**

See you out on the doors,

Peter Hanscom

clean_body: I just checked in, but it looks like you haven’t signed the President’s card yet. There are only 12 hours left before we’re sending him the card..... Friend - I just checked in, but it looks like you haven’t signed the President’s card yet. There are less than 12 hours before we’re sending him the card, friend. Thank you, Leah \-----Begin Forwarded Message----- Friend, One year ago, the American people spoke up against politics as usual and elected the ultimate outsider: Donald Trump. Since then, the media, Democrats, and the Washington elites have teamed up to obstruct the Trump agenda and destroy his reputation. And friend, they still don’t realize that by obstructing the Trump agenda, they’re obstructing OUR agenda. We voted for change. We voted to shake up Washington. And the swamp dwellers have done everything they can to maintain Washington’s dysfunction by blocking efforts to repeal Obamacare, build the wall, and cut taxes. It hasn’t stopped there. At every turn, they’ve relentlessly and personally attacked President Trump. One year in, I want to remind the president that we still stand with him;that the people still have his back. That’s why I’ve created this card, and I’m hoping you will join me in signing it: I am more committed than ever to joining President Trump’s mission to drain the swamp and deliver the America first agenda we voted for. Washington clearly didn’t get the message last November. Let’s make sure they hear it from us now: Enough politics as usual. It’s time to get to work and put America first. Thank you for your support, friend. Together, we can hold Washington accountable in the year to come. Leah P.S. Remember, President Trump is surrounded by the swamp right now. Sign this card to show him real America stands with him: Paid for by Leah for Senate

name: Nina Turner, Our Revolution
subject: This is how we win
body:
#
Friends,
Last night was historic. Our Revolution backed candidates claimed victories at every level of government. In cities, towns, and neighborhoods across America, voters showed up for Election Day 2017 and swept progressive candidates into office.
Our Revolution won 21 seats and one ballot initiative, with more results yet to be finalized. None of this would have been possible without grassroots organizing. A growing and dedicated base of Our Revolution members volunteered to make over **132,000 calls** and send over **373,000 individual text messages** to supporters in key races. Thanks to this massive effort, you've helped elect progressive champions that will be part of the vital work of being responsive to the needs of their constituents and rebuilding our progressive bench from the ground up.
These are the candidates you helped elect:
* Jennifer Carroll Foy, Virginia House of Delegates, District 2
* Elizabeth Guzman, Virginia House of Delegates, District 31
* Lee Carter, Virginia House of Delegates, District 50
* Larry Krasner, Philadelphia District Attorney
* Andrea Jenkins, Minneapolis, Minn. City Council, Ward 8
* Carlos Menchaca, New York City Council, District 38
* Matt McLaughlin, Somerville, Mass. Ward 1 Alderman
* J.T. Scott, Somerville, Mass. Ward 2 Alderman
* Ben Ewen-Campen, Somerville, Mass. Ward 3 Alderman
* Jesse Clingan, Somerville, Mass. Ward 4 Alderman
* Will Mbah, Somerville, Mass. Alderman At-Large
* Jasmin Santana, Cleveland, Ohio City Council, Ward 14
* Tamaya Dennard, Cincinnati, Ohio City Council At-Large
* Tristan Rader, Lakewood, Ohio City Council At-Large
* Gina Morgenstein, Wallingford, Connecticut Town Council
* Ted Terry, Clarkston, Georgia Mayor
* Brian Nowak, Cheektowaga, New York Town Council
* Anita Prizio, Allegheny, Penn. County Council, District 3
* Mik Pappas, Allegheny County, Penn. Magistrate, District 31
* Anna Payne, Middletown, Penn. Township Auditor
* Noelia Corzo, San Mateo/Foster City, California School Board
* Mainers for Healthcare (ballot initiative expanding Medicaid access)

This is only the start. The victories of this election cycle built upon our victories in 2016. This is what this movement is all about. Moment by moment, community by community, election cycle by election, we continue to create a political environment steeped in a commitment to do the work that transforms the lives of people generation after generation. And in 2018, we are going deeper.
As you know, change does not come from Washington. On many of the matters most critical to our movement, the change must happen closer to your community. The candidates you helped to elect are fighting for housing justice, criminal justice, justice in education and justice in political representation. Lives will improve because of you. These are the candidates that will be the next generation of local, state and federal leaders. Just like Bernie Sanders as mayor, newly elected leaders such as Elizabeth Guzman in Virginia, Larry Krasner in Philadelphia, Tamaya Dennard in Cincinnati and the many others you helped elect last night, will shape our future for years to come.
We are looking forward to many more victories in elections ahead. Thank you for being a part of the political revolution.
In solidarity,
Nina Turner
President
Our Revolution
